data.title: CoreWeave's stock soars as Nvidia makes a fresh bet on the company
data.description:
By Britney Nguyen

Nvidia will invest $2 billion more in CoreWeave, which could further stoke concerns about circular financing in the AI ecosystem

CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator said the expanded partnership with Nvidia reflects strong demand.

Shares of CoreWeave were climbing in Monday's premarket trading after Nvidia deepened its bet on the cloud provider.

The companies are expanding their partnership to build out artificial-intelligence data centers more quickly. Additionally, Nvidia (NVDA) is investing $2 billion in CoreWeave's (CRWV) common stock at $87.20 per share.

The cloud infrastructure provider is aiming to build more than five gigawatts' worth of AI data centers by the end of the decade. The data centers, which will be operated by CoreWeave , will run future generations of Nvidia's AI systems, including its upcoming Rubin AI chip platform and Vera central processing units. The data centers will also utilize the chip maker's Bluefield storage systems.

See also: These 5 tech stocks could be big winners this earnings season

"This expanded collaboration underscores the strength of demand we are seeing across our customer base and the broader market signals as AI systems move into large-scale production," CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator said in a statement.

Nvidia was already a major CoreWeave investor, owning about 6.6% of the company's outstanding shares as of the end of September, according to FactSet data.

The latest investment underscores a polarizing aspect of the AI trade. While Nvidia's move could be interpreted as a sign of confidence that the AI era is only just getting started, critics have deemed past moves to be examples of "circular financing," taking issue with how Nvidia has become so intertwined with some of its partners.

CoreWeave's stock is up more than 8% in premarket trades on Monday, while Nvidia's stock is down fractionally.

Don't miss: Micron's business is so hot that profits could quadruple in just two years, says this analyst

-Britney Nguyen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Trump to Send Border Czar Tom Homan to Minnesota — WSJ
data.description:
President Trump said he would send his border czar, Tom Homan, to Minnesota on Monday.

"I am sending Tom Homan to Minnesota tonight," Trump wrote on Truth Social Monday morning. "He has not been involved in that area, but knows and likes many of the people there. Tom is tough but fair, and will report directly to me." The decision comes as some of Trump's advisers have privately expressed concerns that increasingly volatile situation in Minneapolis is becoming a political liability for the White House.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: MakeMyTrip Stock Dropped 5% After Announcement Of Decline In Q3 Profit
data.description:
NEW DELHI (dpa-AFX) - MakeMyTrip Limited's (MMYT) stock fell 5.65 percent, to $62.60 on Friday after the announcement of the third quarter results, which reported a 73.1 percent decline in profit, to $7.3 million, or $0.07 per share, from $27.1 million, or $0.23 per share, last year.

The significant decline in profit was primarily due to higher net finance costs, which jumped to $27.7 million from $4.8 million in the earlier year.

On an adjusted basis, net profit improved to $50.7 million, or $0.52 per share, in the quarter, up 4.7 million from $46.0 million, or $0.39 per share, in the prior year.

Meanwhile, revenue climbed 10.6 percent, to $295.7 million from $267.4 million in the previous year, mainly due to strong travel demand in India for both domestic and international outbound travel.

In the pre-market hours, MMYT is trading at $62.66, up 0.10 percent on the Nasdaq.

data.title: Silver Reaches New High as Investors Chase Hard Assets — Market Talk
data.description:
0852 ET - Silver prices hit a fresh record, riding gold's rally as a weaker U.S. dollar and persistent macroeconomic uncertainty boost investor appetite for hard assets. Futures in New York climb 7.8% to $109.28 a troy ounce after touching an intraday high of $110.92 an ounce. "Silver's strength reflects both resilient safe‑haven flows and robust industrial demand, pushing the gold-silver ratio to just above 50--the lowest level since 2011," ING analysts say, referring to the metric measuring how many ounces of silver it takes to buy an ounce of gold. "Tightening physical balances amid constrained mine‑supply growth are adding further upward pressure." Still, silver's smaller market size and dual role as both an industrial and investment metal leave prices highly volatile, raising the risk of sharp swings, analysts said. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0855 ET - The recent sharp rise in precious-metal prices and concerns about high stock-market valuations could see funds reallocated to cryptocurrencies in any rebalancing of investor portfolios, IG analyst Chris Beauchamp says in a note. "These rebalancing flows could help start a new leg higher for the asset, should the right catalyst arrive." It is also encouraging to see further progress on the U.K.'s Financial Conduct Authority consolation process on crypto regulation, he says. Bitcoin rises 1.4% to $87,680, according to LSEG. Ether rises 2.9% to $2,901. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0846 ET - A frozen Washington is an appropriate setting for a Fed meeting this week that will likely feature central bankers in no hurry to go anywhere fast. "The January FOMC meeting is likely to be uneventful," writes David Mericle of Goldman Sachs. Despite their fervent advocacy for cuts at times in 2025, governors Christopher Waller and Michelle Bowman are likely to back the consensus decision to hold rates steady, leaving governor Stephen Miran as the only probable dissenter, Mericle expects. Looking ahead, Goldman's economics team expects two cuts in 2026--but probably no movement until June. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0839 ET - The prospect of a leadership challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer remains a risk for sterling, MUFG Bank's Lee Hardman says in a note. Andy Burnham was blocked from standing in a local district election which would have opened up a path for him to challenge Starmer. However, the decision could backfire if Labour loses the local district election to Reform and performs poorly in May's wider local elections, Hardman says. That could increase the risk of a leadership challenge from other potential candidates, he says. Local elections pose a threat to sterling and stronger growth is unlikely to prevent the Bank of England from lowering rates further this year if inflation eases closer to target, he says.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0807 ET - President Trump's weekend threats against Canada lack detail and credibility, Scotiabank's Derek Holt argues. The president's threat that Canada could be hit with a 100% tariff on all goods exported to the U.S. if a deal is made with China, if enacted, would be devastating to U.S. businesses across multiple industries and a raft of items wouldn't be economical to supply, Holt says. Add to that the market reaction could echo the "Liberation Day" trade after the April 2025 tariffs, with investors viewing the U.S. to have much higher country risk and selling assets across the board, the economist says. And if carried out there would be enormous pressure for major retaliation, he adds. Still, this instability only adds to the case to diversify away from the U.S. and the share of Canadian exports heading to the U.S. is likely to keep falling over the next decade. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0747 ET - U.K. government bonds and sterling are likely to face increased volatility due to concerns about a possible change of U.K. prime minister as political pressure intensifies, economists at Investec say in a note. Markets fear that a potential change in leadership could lead to a "watering down in the government's commitment to fiscal stability" and higher public borrowing, the economists say. Over the weekend, Andy Burnham, a possible challenger to PM Keir Starmer, was blocked from contesting a local district election, providing temporary relief to the gilt market. Ten-year gilt yields are steady at 4.496% after falling 1.7 basis points early Monday to 4.481%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0702 ET - The cost of default protection for euro credit stays steady as investors exercise caution ahead of this week's U.S. Federal Reserve rate decision and big tech earnings release. Investors expect the Fed to keep rates on hold during Wednesday's decision but will focus on the Fed chair's statement. "Jerome Powell's press conference will be closely scrutinized by investors seeking further insight into the future path for interest rates," ActivTrades Tim Ritschar says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index of euro high-yield credit default swaps is unchanged at 243 basis points, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0644 ET - Investors are increasingly replacing long-dated government bonds--traditionally a stable, low-risk asset--with gold as they look for new ways to hedge equity risk, eToro analyst Lale Akoner writes. "Historically, duration exposure helped cushion drawdowns in risk assets. But recent episodes, where equities and long bonds have sold off in tandem, have undermined confidence in bonds as a reliable hedge at the very moments investors expect them to do the heavy lifting," Akoner writes. Investors are therefore swapping government bond exposure for gold in their portfolios, Akoner says, suggesting "a broader rethink of portfolio risk management" is underway. In New York, gold futures gain 2.2% to $5,088.60 a troy ounce, while silver futures climb 7.6% to $109.10 an ounce. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

0635 ET - Concerns about Japan's loose fiscal policies and high debt position could limit the yen's recovery on speculation over potential joint U.S.-Japanese currency interventions, MUFG Bank's Lee Hardman says in a note. The yen strengthened after reports that the Federal Reserve Bank of New York had contacted potential trading counterparties Friday for rate checks. This fuels speculation about direct interventions to buy the yen and sell the dollar, encouraging speculators to trim bets against the yen, he says. However, "doubts will remain over the sustainability of yen strength given fiscal concerns in Japan haven't been addressed ahead of the coming lower house election on Feb. 8." The dollar falls 1.3% to a two-and-a-half-month low of 153.30 yen, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0635 ET - Investors are increasingly replacing long-dated government bonds--traditionally a stable, low-risk asset--with gold as they look for new ways to hedge equity risk, eToro analyst Lale Akoner writes. "Historically, duration exposure helped cushion drawdowns in risk assets. But recent episodes, where equities and long bonds have sold off in tandem, have undermined confidence in bonds as a reliable hedge at the very moments investors expect them to do the heavy lifting." Investors are therefore swapping government bond exposure for gold in their portfolios, Akoner says, suggesting "a broader rethink of portfolio risk management" is underway. In New York, gold futures gain 2.2% to $5,088.60 a troy ounce, while silver futures climb 7.6% to $109.10 an ounce. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

0610 ET - U.S. Treasury yields have been fairly stable, even as the political environment in the U.S. is unpredictable, analysts at the Investment Institute by UniCredit say in a note. Low volatility in U.S. Treasury yields indicates that investors still favor the bonds, the analysts say. Markets expect the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates further, which is likely to boost Treasurys' performance, they say. In addition, the U.S. economy remains resilient, making U.S. assets attractive, the analysts say. Foreign investors could reduce their investments in U.S. assets over the medium term, but a sharp selloff in the near term is less likely, they say. Ten-year Treasury yields fall 2.8 basis points to 4.211%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0603 ET - Fraying confidence in the dollar is driving precious-metal prices higher, Capital.com market analyst Daniela Hathorn writes. Record gold and silver prices overnight offer "further evidence of a deepening crisis of confidence in the U.S. dollar and the broader monetary order," Hathorn says, with the new highs symptomatic of "a global system under strain." Investors concerned by geopolitical tensions and the Trump administration's interference with Federal Reserve independence are seeking "refuge in assets that sit outside political discretion," she adds, forces that will continue to position precious metals as more than just hedges against risk. In New York, gold futures are up 2.2% at $5,090.30 a troy ounce, while silver climbs 7.9% to $109.34 an ounce. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

data.title: AI frenzy: Business investment has been an unexpected source of strength for the U.S. economy
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

Spending on new technologies drives biggest rise in investment in three years

A robot uses cameras, articulated arms and an on-device AI model to perform household tasks. Here it puts an item of clothing into a washing machine at the annual Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas.

The numbers: Higher U.S. tariffs have dealt a heavy blow to businesses in the past year, but one thing it hasn't done is freeze investment, especially in promising technologies such as robotics and artificial intelligence.

A key measure of business investment rose strongly in November for the fourth time in five months, according to a report delayed by the government shutdown last fall. So-called core orders climbed 0.7%

The increase in these orders - they omit transportation and defense - has topped 5% in the past 12 months and hit the highest level in three years.

While the tariffs have hurt sales and demand for manufactured goods, companies have kept investing in new technologies that have the promise to reshape how they conduct business and even remake the economy.

The surge in investment helped to propel the U.S. economy in 2025 to a surprisingly strong rate of growth, measured by gross domestic product.

Investment is likely to keep up the pace this year, economists say.

Key details: The investment figures were included in the government's monthly report on durable goods - items meant to last a long time.

Orders for durable goods jumped 5.3% in the month, but the increase was exaggerated by new contracts for passenger planes. These orders take a few years to fulfill, and they can skew the report in the short run.

A better way to glean underlying demand for manufactured goods is to strip out airplanes and autos. Orders minus transportation rose a more modest 0.5%.

Big picture: The effects of tariffs are expected to fade this year and give manufacturers a boost. Investment in artificial intelligence is also sure to stay strong.

All of this could help the U.S. economy to expand for the fifth straight year at an above-average rate of growth.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX were set to decline in Monday trading.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: Manchester City And Ohana Development sign agreement to launch club-branded residential destination in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
• World’s first club-branded residential destination ‘Manchester City Yas Residences by Ohana’ to be developed in Abu Dhabi

Manchester City has partnered with Ohana Development, a leading real estate developer in the UAE, to launch ‘Manchester City Yas Residences by Ohana’, a first-of-its-kind Manchester City club-branded residential destination.

The development will be set along Abu Dhabi’s Yas Canal waterfront and span 1.67 million square metres. Further details will be revealed as the project progresses. 

Ohana Development, which will design and develop the development, is renowned for collaborating with leading brands across culture, harnessing their appeal into high-end residential environments that reflect Ohana’s luxury-led design ethos, with the Manchester City brand integrated throughout the development.

Through this project, Ohana will leverage Manchester City’s unmatched presence in Abu Dhabi. Each term, more than 3,500 children participate in City Football Schools sessions, delivered five days a week across seven locations by over 50 full-time accredited coaches. The Manchester City Abu Dhabi Cup is the largest youth football tournament in the region, bringing together thousands of players from across the UAE and globally each year. Beyond the pitch, Manchester City drives positive impact through community and educational initiatives such as the Healthy Lifestyles Programme, engaging over 30,000 primary school children last year to promote healthier, more active lifestyles.

Ferran Sorriano, Chief Executive Officer of City Football Group, said: “This is a landmark project that brings Manchester City to a premium residential environment in a distinctively club-branded way. Ohana Development is highly regarded for working with leading global brands, translating their influence into high-end, design-driven residential experiences. This partnership will see Ohana harness the Club’s global reach and its unrivalled footprint in the region. We continue to be committed to distinctive collaborations like this.”

Husein Salem, CEO of Ohana Development, said: “Manchester City Yas Residences by Ohana brings this shared vision to life through a thoughtfully curated residential project, one that is set to become a lasting legacy within the real estate sector. Partnering with Manchester City marks a significant milestone for Ohana Development and reflects our long-standing commitment to Abu Dhabi, where we are proudly headquartered. The club’s global stature, forward-looking vision, and modern outlook closely align with our ambition to create future-ready communities that contribute to the emirate’s evolving lifestyle landscape.”

About Manchester City Football Club:  

Manchester City FC was initially founded in 1880 as St Mark’s West Gorton and officially became Manchester City FC in 1894. Situated on the wider Etihad Campus, the Club’s footprint includes the 53,000 capacity Etihad Stadium, the 7,000 capacity Joie Stadium and City Football Academy, a state-of-the-art performance, training and youth development facility home to the Club’s men’s, women’s and academy teams.   

Ranked as the Most Valuable Football Club Brand in the Premier League by Brand Finance, Manchester City FC is currently developing a best-in-class fan experience and year-round entertainment and leisure destination at the Etihad Campus. The Club is committed to operating in a sustainable and socially responsible manner and ensures that equality, diversity and inclusion is embedded into its decision-making processes, culture and practices.  

For more information, please visit www.mancity.com  

About Ohana Development:

Ohana Development is a leading private developer in the Middle East, specialising in the conceptualisation, design, and development of attainable luxury residences in prime locations, thoughtfully crafted to meet the unique aspirations of its clients. True to its name, which means "family" in Hawaiian, Ohana embodies a family spirit, creating a sense of belonging and connection in every community it builds. Guided by core values of customer centricity, attainable luxury, and personalisation, Ohana ensures that every project, from serene beachfronts and canal views to hillside homes and futuristic concepts, enhances lifestyle value through a seamless blend of modernity and exclusivity.

With over 35 years of expertise, the company is dedicated to delivering exceptional homes that blend luxury, quality, and value. Operating across four regional offices and supported by a team of over 2,000 employees, the company has developed an impressive portfolio of 9,000+ residential units, in real estate value. By ensuring every customer becomes part of the Ohana family, the company delivers properties that enrich lives and create a lasting legacy for generations.

Renowned for its world-class properties, Ohana’s flagship developments, the ‘Jacob & Co. Beachfront Living by Ohana’, the ELIE SAAB Waterfront by Ohana, and ‘Ohana by the Sea’, located in the UAE, exemplifies the brand’s promise to create sophisticated, unique spaces. Other signature projects in Lebanon, include Ohana Villas, featuring bespoke designs from the ELIE SAAB Maison collection and Ohana Hills, offering breathtaking views. These developments highlight Ohana Development’s commitment to providing unparalleled, client-focused lifestyle experiences, establishing the company as a leader in the UAE and internationally.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY

A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE

Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

1.         KEY INFORMATION

(a) Full name of discloser:

State Street Global Advisors & Affiliates

(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):

data.title: Kinder Morgan Elba LNG export plant undergoing maintenance
data.description:
U.S. energy firm Kinder Morgan's said on Monday that its Elba Island liquefied natural gas export plant in Georgia is experiencing scheduled maintenance for a system upgrade.

"The MMLS (Moveable Modular Liquefaction System) Units should be back up by the weekend," a company spokesperson said in an email to Reuters.

data.title: IONQ: $1.8B acquisition forms a full-stack quantum platform, accelerating secure U.S. quantum innovation
data.description:
The acquisition creates a vertically integrated quantum platform, combining advanced quantum technology with secure, onshore manufacturing. The $1.8B deal accelerates the path to fault-tolerant quantum computing, enhances U.S. supply chain security, and is expected to close in Q2–Q3 2026.

Original document: IonQ, Inc. [IONQ] Slides Release — Jan. 26 2026

data.title: Lloyds Banking Group Subsidiary Fined for Russian Sanctions Breach
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

A subsidiary of lender Lloyds Banking Group was fined 160,000 pounds ($218,592) by the U.K. for breaching Russia sanction regulations.

The U.K.'s Office of Financial Sanctions Implementation on Monday said that it imposed a penalty on Bank of Scotland for processing payments in February 2023 to or from a personal current account held at its trading division Halifax Bank by a sanctioned individual.

Lloyds reported the breaches voluntarily on behalf of Bank of Scotland, meaning that a discount was applied to the original 320,000-pound fine.

"We acted swiftly and transparently, proactively referring this one-off, isolated matter to OFSI and working closely with them throughout," said a Lloyds spokesperson, adding that the bank has strengthened its controls since the breach.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Key Metric in Delivery Hero's Korean Unit Likely Returned to Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Delivery Hero's Korean segment likely returned to growth in its gross merchandise value metric and stabilized its market share in 2025, Jefferies analysts Giles Thorne and Weng Lum Khoo write in a note. GMV represents the total value of all transactions made on the platform. The German food-delivery company's Middle East unit, Talabat, is insulated from disruption related to Chinese rival Meituan's expansion, thanks to its strategy and execution, they add. Additionally, Delivery Hero's activist investor is expected to push for further asset sales across the portfolio and cost savings, they say. The U.S. bank cuts its 2025-27 revenue forecasts by around 2% and reduces its adjusted Ebitda estimates by roughly 2% to 4%, reflecting a change in foreign-exchange assumptions. The company will publish its 2025 results on Feb. 27. Shares are up 0.7% at 25.89 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Correction to SLB Readies Rapid Expansion in Venezuela — 2nd Update on Jan. 23
data.description: SLB is expected to report annual revenue of $37.25 billion in 2026, according to the consensus of 26 analysts polled by FactSet. "SLB Readies Rapid Expansion in Venezuela — 2nd Update" on Jan. 23 at 1.26 p.m. ET incorrectly said that SLB's full-year revenue outlook came in below the $38.83 billion that Wall Street modeled.

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Soars. Why Nvidia Is Investing $2 Billion in the AI Cloud Company. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Artificial-intelligence cloud company CoreWeave surged early Monday on news of an expanded partnership with Nvidia, which includes a $2 billion investment from the chip maker.

Nvidia invested in CoreWeave class A common stock at a purchase price of $87.20 per share. CoreWeave shares were up 10.8% at $103 in premarket trading.

The two companies said the agreement will enable CoreWeave to accelerate the buildout of more than five gigawatts of "AI factories" by 2030.

"CoreWeave's deep AI factory expertise, platform software, and unmatched execution velocity are recognized across the industry. Together, we're racing to meet extraordinary demand for NVIDIA AI factories — the foundation of the AI industrial revolution," Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said in a statement.

CoreWeave is the largest of the so-called "neoclouds" which provide cloud-computing capacity for AI and has built a revenue backlog of $55.6 billion through deals with OpenAI, Meta Platforms, and others. It has close ties to Nvidia, which provides access to advanced hardware.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Kyung Hee University Secures 5th Place Among Top Universities in South Korea and Climbs to 252nd Globally in THE World Rankings
data.description:
Seoul, South Korea--(Newsfile Corp. - January 26, 2026) - Kyung Hee University has solidified its place as one of the top universities in South Korea, securing 5th place among domestic comprehensive universities and reaching 252nd globally in the 2026 Times Higher Education (THE) World University Rankings.

Kyung Hee University

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8871/281242_368694b644909b71_001full.jpg

This milestone reflects a continued rise in global academic competitiveness and research excellence, as Kyung Hee moved up 33 places from the previous year. The result affirms the university's long-term commitment to becoming a "true university of the future."

Strong Research Performance in Both Quality and Quantity

According to THE metrics, Kyung Hee saw improvements across multiple research indicators. The Field-Weighted Citation Impact (FWCI) rose from 1.37 in 2019 to 1.42 in 2024, meaning its papers are cited 42% more frequently than the global average.

Notably, papers ranked in the top 25% of citations showed even greater gains, with FWCI climbing from 1.36 to 1.54, placing the university third in Korea in this metric. Total publications also increased significantly, from 16,023 in 2019 to 20,934 in 2024, and the share of highly cited papers grew from 11.5% to 14.2%.

Multi-Disciplinary Global Recognition

Kyung Hee's strengths span medicine, engineering, natural sciences, and basic science. Its medical teams are leading global research on oncology, geriatrics, and digital health, while engineering departments are advancing in semiconductors, optoelectronics, robotics, and precision systems.

In basic science, Kyung Hee researchers have published high-impact work in biology, environment, and ecology, forming a research ecosystem that bridges foundational theory with practical innovation.

A Leader in Global Collaboration

International cooperation has become a strategic asset. The university ranked 2nd in Korea for global engagement and saw international co-authored research grow from 29.8% to 38.1% in just five years. These projects achieved an impressive FWCI of 2.63, signaling world-class impact.

Noteworthy initiatives include the International Center for Quantum Matter, established with global experts, joining forces with Kyung Hee's physics and engineering faculty.

Looking Ahead: Building a "University of the Future"

Kyung Hee University now focuses on AI-integrated innovation and long-term infrastructure. Under the AI Transition (AX) initiative, the university is developing campus-wide AI governance and launching a new Institutional Research (IR) Center to support data-informed strategy.

The next 3-4 years have been designated as a pivotal "golden period" for transformation, reinforcing Kyung Hee's ambition to bridge the gap with elite global institutions.

https://www.khu.ac.kr/eng/user/main/view.do

About Kyung Hee University 

Established in 1949, Kyung Hee University is a prestigious private comprehensive university in South Korea, recently ranking 5th among domestic private universities in the 2026 THE World University Rankings. Renowned for its world-class research, KHU is rapidly rising as a global hub for academic innovation.

Contact

Minjae Jeong

communication@khu.ac.kr

+82-2-961-0106

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/281242

data.title: Canada rules out China trade deal after Trump tariff threat
data.description:
The Canadian prime minister said on Sunday that the country respected its trade deal with the US and Mexico, which prohibits it from pursuing free-trade agreements with non-market economies without prior notification.

He told reporters: "We have no intention of doing that with China or any other non-market economy."

His comments were later echoed by Dominic LeBlanc, minister for Canada-US trade, who posted on X: "There is no pursuit of a free trade deal with China.

"Canada’s new government is building a stronger Canadian economy, with a plan that is building our strength at home and strengthening our trading partnerships throughout the world."

The comments follow a period of heightened tension between Ottawa and Washington.

Canada and China agreed to remove trade barriers on electric vehicles just over a week ago. Trump initially seem unperturbed, commenting on social media: "If you can get a deal with China, you should do that."

But by Saturday his tone had changed. Taking to social media again, he warned: "If Governor Carney thinks he is going to make Canada a drop-off port for China to send goods and products in the United States he is sorely mistaken.

"If Canada makes a deal with China, it will immediately be hit with a 100 per cent tariffs against all Canadian goods and products."

Trump regularly refers to Canadian prime ministers as governors, in reference to his claims that Canada should become the 51st US state.

Carney angered Trump last week in Davos, after the former head of the Bank of England delivered a highly-praised speech warning that the rules-based international order was undergoing a "rupture". Trump was not mentioned by name but the speech was seen as a thinly-veiled attack on the US president.

In his own speech at the World Economic Forum, Trump hit back, warning: "Canada lives because of the United States. Remember that Mark, the next time you make your statements."
